Each of the eight themed ports features attractions, restaurants, shops, and more, inviting guests to a world “Where Adventure and Imagination Set Sail!” ・Mediterranean Harbor: A romantic Southern European port town ・American Waterfront: The nostalgia-filled American harbors of New York and Cape Cod ・Port Discovery: A futuristic marina located across the horizons of time ・Lost River Delta: The ruins of an ancient civilization in the Central American jungles ・Fantasy Springs: Magical springs that lead to worlds of Disney fantasy ・Arabian Coast: The world of The Arabian Nights filled with magic and mystery ・Mermaid Lagoon: An undersea kingdom home to Ariel, the little mermaid, and her friends ・Mysterious Island: The eccentric genius Captain Nemo's secret base (1.) description:
Carry passengers 950 meters (3,116 feet), making it the highest vertical aerial tramway in the country Soars through the clouds to provide views of Japan's highest mountain- Mt. Fuji, as well as the seven Izu Island and expansive coastline Lead through the forest with limited panoramic views of surrounding scenery and can get closed when there's volcanic activity Several attractions are located in the area around the ropeway's lower station at the shores of Lake Ashinoko, known as Hakone-en “Hakone Komagatake Ropeway” is a sightseeing ropeway that connects from Hakone-en to the height of Hakone Komagatake. You can casually enjoy the magnificent prospect seeing from the ropeway on the highest place of Japan that only climber used to enjoy. The whole length is 1,783 meters, and it takes about seven minutes one way on the mountain slope that seasonal alpine flora blooms. We recommend the height of Mt. Komagatake, that is full of attraction of nature such as celestial observations or hiking course, not only for the people who want to see the magnificent prospect but beginners of Hakone sightseeing. What you can see from Sancho station (summit station) of 1,327 meters above sea level is the panoramic view of Hakone such as Lake Ashino-ko. Beautiful panoramic views like Mt. Fuji, Suruga bay and Miura Peninsula in far distance widespread on good weather day. There is observation deck and “Hakone Mototsumiya” known as shrine pavilion of sky on the top of Mt. Hakone Komagatake, so please enjoy the view.

title: Tokyo Mt. Fuji & Hakone Full-Day Guided Tour(Return by Motorcoach)
seller_title: Tokyo Mt. Fuji & Hakone Full-Day Guided Tour(Return by Motorcoach)
description:
Discover majestic Mt. Fuji , a UNESCO World Heritage site, and the stunning Hakone area on an unforgettable day trip from Tokyo! Your adventure begins departing Shinjuku, heading towards the iconic mountain. Journey up the scenic Subaru Line to Mt. Fuji's 5th Station (2300m) for breathtaking panoramic views (weather permitting). Afterwards, savor an authentic traditional Japanese lunch (if meal plan selected; please advise of dietary needs at booking). Then, experience the volcanic wonders of Ōwakudani valley , and conclude with a spectacular Hakone Ropeway descent offering stunning vistas, leading to a picturesque cruise across beautiful Lake Ashi. A truly immersive Japanese experience awaits!

description: The visit to Golden Pavilion is limited to tours without meals included. Embark on an enriching journey through Kyoto and Nara prefecture. Begin at VIP Villa Namba or Kyoto VIP Lounge. Marvel at the iconic vermilion torii gates as you stroll through Mount Inari's forest. The first stop is Arashiyama. In addition to enjoying the beautiful scenery of the four seasons, Arashiyama is also famous for its bamboo forest paths formed by towering green bamboos and the beautiful Togetsukyo Bridge. Then, visit Kinkakuji, once the shogun's villa, now a Zen temple. Nara Park awaits, home to over 1,200 deer and Tōdai-ji Temple, a breathtaking World Heritage site. Conclude your tour with a return bus journey to Kyoto or Osaka, cherishing the memories of Japan's cultural and natural wonders.

seller_title: Mount Fuji One-Day Tour: Kawaguchiko Tenjozan Park with Ropeway & Arakurayama Sengen Park & Nichikawa Clock Shop & Lawson Convenience Store & Oishi Park (Departing from Tokyo)
additional_details:

description:
Kawaguchiko Tenjo-yama Park | A superb vantage point to overlook Mount Fuji and the lake Located at the highest point of Lake Kawaguchiko, take the Tenjo-yama Ropeway directly to the observation deck to take in the full view of Mount Fuji and the scenery of Lake Kawaguchiko. Looking out from above, every frame is a stunning and healing natural painting. Kawaguchiko Oishi Park | The Four Seasons Fairy Tale of Mount Fuji Sitting by the lake, gazing at the perpetually snow-capped peak of Mount Fuji. Cherry blossoms in spring, purple lavender in summer, autumn leaves, and winter snow—the most beautiful aspects of all four seasons are hidden here. Mount Arakura Sengen Park | A photographer's secret Mount Fuji viewing spot Climb to the observation deck to capture the grandeur of Mount Fuji and the Chureito Pagoda in the same frame. Cherry blossoms cover the mountains in spring, and red leaves dye them in autumn; every season is an unrepeatable wonderland. Fujiyoshida Old Town | A Sky Ladder Town Walking in the Clouds Stroll through the streets filled with Showa-era charm, and look up to see the majestic Mount Fuji. Retro shops, nostalgic street scenes, and distant mountains complement each other, with every corner exuding a gentle Japanese ambiance. Mount Fuji Lawson Convenience Store | A Dreamy Collision of Modernity and Nature This convenience store beneath the snowy mountain has become a viral check-in landmark across the internet. Every casual shot is a Japanese-style blockbuster, capturing the most special moments of Mount Fuji. One trip, multiple experiences! These hidden gem attractions are strung together to create a beautiful Mount Fuji journey, allowing you to immerse yourself in the romance and soul of Mount Fuji amidst natural splendor, millennia of heritage, and the hustle and bustle of human life.

title: Osaka: Sumo Experience with Live Show & Audience Challenge(Ticket)
seller_title: Osaka: Sumo Experience with Live Show & Audience Challenge(Ticket)
description:
Sumo is considered Japan’s national sport, and some even say its history is older than Japan itself. It began as a Shinto ritual, inherited the spirit of Bushido, and still remains a vibrant part of Japanese culture today. In this show, you’ll experience the power, tradition, and excitement of sumo up close. We’ll guide you through its rich history, sacred rituals, and signature techniques. During the demonstration, you’ll see authentic sumo training, famous techniques (kimarite), and an intense one-round match (tachiai) between retired sumo wrestlers. For those who wish, there’s even a chance to step into the ring and challenge a sumo wrestler yourself (limited spots available).

title: Osaka Dotonbori Naniwa Sightseeing Boat
seller_title: Osaka Dotonbori Naniwa Sightseeing Boat
description:
Experience the most iconic waterfront charm of Osaka. Dotonbori has been a bustling hub of theater and entertainment since the Edo period. Today, it still retains its vibrant neon signs, festive atmosphere, and enticing aromas of delicious food. By taking the Dotonbori River Cruise, you will see the most authentic side of Osaka from the river's perspective: you can admire many famous landmarks and also feel the daily life of the local residents. The vitality of the day and the lights of the night each present a different charm, quietly awaiting your arrival. Whether you are a first-time visitor to Osaka or someone looking to delve deeper into Japanese culture, this cruise will create unforgettable memories.

title: Kyoto Traditional Japanese Tea Ceremony Culture & Matcha Tasting Experience(Women's Kimono)
seller_title: Kyoto Traditional Japanese Tea Ceremony Culture & Matcha Tasting Experience(Women's Kimono)
additional_details: Eligibility For experience and safety considerations, children aged 0-9 are temporarily unable to participate in tea ceremony activities.
description:
Opening Introduction: The tea ceremony master will introduce the history, cultural connotations, and basic etiquette of the Japanese tea ceremony, giving you a preliminary understanding of it. Tea Ceremony Demonstration: The master will perform a complete tea ceremony demonstration, showcasing a series of actions such as boiling water, warming bowls, preparing matcha powder, brewing tea, and stirring, allowing you to experience the ritualistic beauty of the tea ceremony. Hands-on Experience: Under the guidance of the master, you will personally experience the process of making tea, from grinding matcha powder to whisking up a delicate foam, every step is full of fun. Tea Tasting and Exchange: Taste the matcha you made yourself, paired with wagashi (Japanese sweets), and exchange tea tasting experiences with other participants, sharing your insights into tea culture. Important Notes: Pregnancy Restriction: Due to the constraints of kimono attire and the kneeling etiquette required for the tea ceremony, pregnant women cannot be provided with kimono dressing or tea ceremony experience services. Gentle Reminder: The matcha experience requires a quiet atmosphere and is not suitable for infants and young children. We appreciate your understanding.

title: LEGOLAND Discovery Center Tokyo Ticket
seller_title: LEGOLAND Discovery Center Tokyo Ticket
description: Legoland has captured the spirit of adventure of millions of people around the world! Legoland Discovery Center Tokyo opens its doors to bring you to an amazing world full of these colorful, interlocking building bricks. It's the perfect day for creativity, building, and adventure especially for kids, fostering their creative minds and urging them to build and create. Considered as the Ultimate Indoor Lego Playground, you'll get to visit zones that allow you to build and play with the world famous bricks, and even get hop onto 3 of its amusement rides. Make a little city of your own in the Lego City Builder or see the world's capitals in a miniaturized form in Miniland! Take a tour of the Lego Factory and see how the bricks are made or head to the Duplo Village, which is perfect for very young kids. title: Kyoto: Private Highlights Tour with Guide and Luxury Vehicle
seller_title: Kyoto: Private Highlights Tour with Guide and Luxury Vehicle
additional_details:
What to bring
Comfortable shoes
Not allowed
Alcohol and drugs
Alcoholic drinks in the vehicle
description:
Embark on an exceptional sightseeing tour of Kyoto, carefully customized to accommodate your specific interests and preferences. Our courteous and knowledgeable private driver-guide is committed to ensuring that your experience is both enjoyable and educational, guiding you through renowned landmarks and hidden gems while offering insightful commentary on Kyoto's rich history, architecture, and vibrant culture.

The tour begins with visits to the serene bamboo groves of Arashiyama, the historic Tenryu-ji Temple and its tranquil garden, the picturesque Togetsu-kyo Bridge, and the Monkey Park Iwatayama located on Arashiyama’s hillside. You will also explore the enchanting Kimono Forest, famous for its colorful Kimono fabric-wrapped pillars.

Next, we head to the iconic Kinkaku-ji, the Golden Pavilion, renowned for its stunning reflecting pond and meticulous gardens. Afterward, enjoy a leisurely lunch at your favorite restaurant or at Nishi-Ki Market, where a variety of eateries offer local specialties and international cuisine, allowing you to explore Kyoto’s diverse culinary scene.

Following lunch, visit the historic Kiyomizu-dera Temple, celebrated for its wooden stage offering panoramic views over the city. Then, wander through the charming Ninenzaka and Sannenzaka Streets, lined with traditional shops and tea houses. Continue to the Gion district, famous for its historic teahouses and geisha culture.

Finally, visit Fushimi Inari Taisha, renowned for its thousands of vibrant red torii gates that wind through the sacred mountain shrine. If time permits, we can include additional sites based on your interests. Throughout the tour, you can customize the itinerary according to your preferences, and your guide will gladly follow your plan to ensure a memorable experience.
To ensure your safety and comfort, we provide professional staff trained in customer service and safety protocols, along with luxury vehicles that meet the highest standards of quality and comfort for the luxury tour experience. Additionally, we offer complementary pick-up services from Kyoto, Osaka, and Nara. No other tour company provides free pick-up and drop-off services outside the Kyoto area, making our service more convenient and comprehensive.
exclusions:
Food and drinks
Entry tickets
images: [{img_url=A12, img_alt=Kyoto: Private Highlights Tour with Guide and Luxury Vehicle}]
inclusions:
Hotel pickup and drop-off
English-speaking guide
Private transportation in an air-conditioned vehicle
Wi-Fi on board

title: TeamLab Planets TOKYO Ticket(Evening Slot)
seller_title: TeamLab Planets TOKYO Ticket(Evening Slot)
additional_details:
Opeing Hours : Mon - Sun(08:30 - 22:00)
  Please note that all participants must enter the attractions together under the same booking. Separate entry is not allowed
Tickets can only be used on the selected date and cannot be used outside the selected dates. You cannot enter without a voucher
Please note you may have to wait 30-60 minutes before being admitted into the venue. Please be patient if any delays occur
Participants with wheelchairs will be requested to transfer to the venue's available wheelchairs once inside. The number of available wheelchairs is limited. Please take note that the wheelchairs are the standard type, without any special accessories
In some areas, you will need to go barefoot to experience the artwork. Please remove your shoes before entering the locker space. Water levels can rise to knee height for adults
Small children are recommended to wear clothes that they don't mind getting wet, or to bring a change of clothes for them
If your clothing gets wet, the staff will ask you to change. For safety reasons, guests are not allowed entry into the next artwork with wet clothes. If you do not have a change of clothes, there are towels available to rent. Please wipe off your wet clothes before entering the next artwork
Please note that an area of the exhibit has a mirror surface. Your underwear may reflect on the mirror surface if you wear a skirt or wide-hemmed pants. It is highly recommended to wear pants during the experience
The facility is not liable for any participants' injury, loss, or other problems occurring inside the venue
Due to the facility's intricate layout, pets, including guide dogs, are prohibited from entering
Lockers for small luggage are available to use and free of charge. Lockers are 23 cm wide, 34 cm deep, and 37 cm high
Please note that some artworks display flashing lights and loud noises
Limited Admissions: teamLab Planets TOKYO will sell “pre-order” electronic tickets in 30-minute time slots with a specified date and time to ease visitor congestion. In addition, we are limiting the number of tickets available for each time slot and restricting admission to avoid overcrowding once we reopen
The exhibition will be open to the public in case of light rain. The exhibition may be canceled in case of strong rain with strong winds such as typhoons
Tickets can only be used on the dates selected and cannot be used outside the selected dates
Due to safety reasons, certain areas are not accessible to pregnant women
For visitors with children, please ensure you hold their hand at all times to avoid any potential danger
Please note that video or photo recording at the site may take place without prior notice
Strollers are not allowed inside. Please leave your stroller in the designated area before entering
For other details, including the latest terms of use and precautions, please refer to the 
Re-entry is not allowed after you leave the venue
Outside food and drinks are not allowed inside the venue
Dangerous materials and items such as bottles, firecrackers, lasers, and other things that may disturb or inconvenience other attendees are prohibited inside the venue
Smoking and drinking is not allowed. You will immediately be asked to leave if caught by a staff member
Photography is allowed, but taking videos and photos for commercial purposes, as well as the live broadcasting of the show is strictly prohibited
description: Explore teamLab Planets TOKYO, where you can be part of the art! Created by art collective teamLab, this museum invites you to explore massive artwork spaces like the Floating Flower Garden and the Collecting Extinct Forest
exclusions:
Transport

Meals and beverages

description:
Kyoto and Nara, Japan: A Twin-City Journey Through Ancient Capitals, Immersing Yourself in the Allure of World Heritage Sites 

Step into the ancient Japanese capitals of Kyoto and Nara, and you'll feel as though you've traveled into a living, breathing historical scroll. Here, history intertwines with modernity, and tradition collides with innovation. Every stone-paved road and every shrine carries within it a thousand years of stories .

Nara: A Wonderful Experience Dancing with Deer 

Nara is a city with deer as its soul. In Nara Park, you can get up close and personal with thousands of wild, adorable deer, feeding them, taking photos, and feeling their gentle eyes and affectionate demeanor .These deer are not only envoys of the city but also witnesses to its history .

Fushimi Inari-taisha Shrine: The Stunning Beauty of a Thousand Torii Gates 

Fushimi Inari-taisha Shrine is a pilgrimage site for millions of worshipers in Japan. The seemingly endless rows of red torii gates form a spectacular corridor of a thousand torii gates. Sunlight shines through the gaps between the torii gates, as if stepping into a mysterious world .

Arashiyama: A Perfect Fusion of Nature and Culture 

Arashiyama is Kyoto's green treasure, home to both ancient temples and shrines , as well as magnificent natural scenery. Take the Sagano Scenic Railway  through Arashiyama, enjoying the beautiful scenery that changes with the seasons along the way. The Arashiyama Bamboo Grove is a paradise for photography enthusiasts. Strolling through it, you'll feel as if you're immersed in a sea of bamboo
